videos.title: MARCIA GRAN PARADISO 2014 - Race Day - The ultimate Film!
videos.description:
[ITA] - Marcia Gran Paradiso a Wibault e Pessey
Il poliziotto di Chamonix Mathias Wibault arresta... la corsa di Hamoumraoui (FRA) e Hjelmeset (NOR). Iris Pessey (FRA) inarrestabile, domina la gara rosa su Hvittfeld (SWE) e Martinsen (NOR). MarciaGranParadiso Light tutta tricolore con Elisa Brocard e Mathias Trento. All'arrivo l'alpina Brocard si è commossa nel dedicare la vittoria all'ex azzurra cogneins Joelle Cuneaz.

videos.title: MARCIAGRANPARADISO Cogne - Course Reconnaissance
videos.description:
Check: http://www.marciagranparadiso.it

[ITA] - La MarciaGranParadiso è una gara di sci di fondo, organizzata con cadenza annuale a Cogne (AO), Valle d'Aosta. La manifestazione nacque nel 1975 grazie alla lungimiranza di dieci temerari cognein i quali diedero la possibilità a tutti i partecipanti di godere dell'incredibile natura e degli splendidi panorami del Parco Nazionale del Gran Paradiso. 
Oggi la MarciaGranParadiso si svolge su tre giorni e propone due gare da 45 e 25 km, la Classic e la Skating dedicate agli adulti, e una gara dedicata ai più giovani, la Minimarcia. Dal 2016 l'evento è entrato a far parte del circuito internazionale Euroloppet.

[ENG] - MarciaGranParadiso is a cross-country skiing competition, which takes place every year in Cogne, Aosta Valley. This event was born in 1975 thanks to the vision of 10 daring people of cogne which gave the opportunity to all participants to enjoy the incredible nature and the stunning landscapes of Gran Paradiso National Park.
Today MarciaGranParadiso takes place over three days and offers two competitions, the Skating and Classic, both of 45 and 25 km,  dedicated to adults, and a race dedicated to the youngest, the so-called Minimarcia. Since 2016 the event has become part of the Euroloppet international circuit.

videos.title: Cross-country skiing in Cogne, Aosta
videos.description: Cogne in Aosta Valley (Italy): an Alpine Pearls member and cross-country skiing paradise

videos.title: MarciaGranParadiso 2026 | Epic Drone Highlights from Cogne [4K]
videos.description:
On February 1st, 2026, the 43rd edition of the MarciaGranParadiso once again transformed the Prati di Sant’Orso in Cogne into one of the most iconic stages of cross-country skiing.

This video captures the event from a privileged perspective — from above. The aerial footage, shot by Leonardo Imbimbo Roullet with a DJI Mavic Mini 4, offers a breathtaking view of the start, the movement of the athletes across the snow, and the spectacular alpine setting that frames the race. The editing, curated by Nicolò Caneparo of Nickland Media, enhances the rhythm, the light, and the emotion of an unforgettable day.

The participants of the MarciaGranParadiso are known as the “bisonti” — the bison — a name that perfectly embodies the strength, endurance, and determination required to take part in this legendary Nordic ski gran fondo. On this special day, they had the privilege of skiing across the historic Prati di Sant’Orso, a venue that has witnessed the efforts and triumphs of countless champions of cross-country skiing.

From the early morning light casting long shadows across the snow to the powerful collective movement of hundreds of athletes gliding in unison, this edition once again celebrated sport in its purest form. The MarciaGranParadiso is not only a race; it is a ritual, a gathering of passion, resilience, and alpine tradition in the heart of the Gran Paradiso National Park.

These images tell a story of motion and silence, of breath and effort, of mountains and community. They reveal the scale of the event, the beauty of Cogne, and the unique atmosphere that makes the MarciaGranParadiso one of the most evocative cross-country skiing experiences in the Alps.

Sit back and relive the 43rd MarciaGranParadiso from a new perspective — from the sky.

----------

The background Music is Zero Hour created by Letrafrom, from Artlist.io.
License Owner: Nicolò Caneparo - Pro License Number: Wu7mK6

----------

#drone #marciagranparadiso #cogne

videos.title: Minimarciagranparadiso e Vitalini Nordic Speed Contest 2020
videos.description:
È stata un’autentica festa degli sci stretti oggi - sabato - sui Prati di Sant’Orso di Cogne, presi d’assalto da quasi 400 giovani fondisti che hanno partecipato alla Mini MarciaGranParadiso, manifestazione del calendario Asiva organizzata dallo sci club Gran Paradiso. Un pomeriggio di sano agonismo e di grande divertimento a Cogne, dove si è svolto anche il Vitalini Nordic Speed Contest.

Baby Sprint - Nella gara femminile primo posto per Jacqueline Bagno (Team Ski Torgnon 2.0) che ha concluso in 4’31”, 14” più veloce di Sophie Bionaz (Godioz) e 21” di Viola Bochicchio (Saint-Barthélemy). Al maschile successo di Noah Desayeux (Amis de Verrayes; 4’01”) che ha preceduto di 27” Aksel Chabod (Valsavarenche) e di 31” Matteo Donato (Gressoney).

Baby - Vittoria di Elody Vittaz (Amis de Verrayes) in 6’15”, un secondo meglio di Leticia Busca (Gressoney) e 6” di Arline Chabod (Valsavarenche). Enea Bortolotti (Amis de Verrayes) si è aggiudicato la gara maschile in 5’44”, 7” davanti a Pietro Quattrone e 14” a Michel Gontier (Drink). 

Cuccioli - Gradino alto del podio per Chiara Quattrone (Fallère) in 9’46”, con secondo e terzo posto per Martina Bisson (Pollein +1”) e Amelie Fragno (Pollein; + 23”). Tripletta degli Amis de Verrayes al maschile, con Teseo Bortolotti (8’23”) che ha preceduto di 6” Jody Vittaz e di 11” Cedric Nex. 

Ragazzi - Ennesimo successo stagionale per Vittoria Cena (Godioz; 13’21”) che ha fatto il vuoto e lasciato a 40” Asia Meynet (Amis de Verrayes) e a 48” Martina Tullia Trentin (Drink). La gara maschile è andata a Daniel Maluquin Segor (Drink), primo in 15’07”, 4” davanti a Edoardo Pesce (Godioz) e 48” a Jacopo Florio (Godioz).

Allievi - Prima posizione per Virginia Cena (Godioz) in 16’12”, seguita a 10” da Irene Negrin (Prali Val Germanasca) e a 1’09” da Giulia Leonardo (Amis de Verrayes). Al maschile successo di Simone Gemelli (Godioz) in 21’46” che ha prevalso su Leonardo Scapinello (Cunardo +3”) e Tommaso Cuc (Gran Paradiso), staccato di 13”.

Subito dopo la gara di fondo, spazio al Vitalini Nordic Speed Contest. Nei Baby Sprint, i più veloci nella prova in discesa con sci da fondo ai piedi, sono stati Smilla Mariotti Cavagnet e Noah Desayeux, nei Baby primi Sophie Bonin e Mattia Carnesecca, nei Cuccioli vittorie di Emilie Beltrami e Jody Vittaz, nei Ragazzi successi di Nayeli Mariotti Cavagnet e Daniel Maluquin Segor, mentre negli Allievi hanno primeggiato Giulia Leonardo e Simone Gemelli.

Domani il grande giorno della MarciaGranParadiso nel suo format tradizionale. Cogne ospiterà la 40 chilometri in tecnica classica, oltre a una versione più corta di 25 chilometri. Due gare del circuito Euroloppet che chiuderanno tre giornate dedicate al fondo. Partenza alle 10 sui Prati di Sant’Orso, premiazioni alle 15. 

Riprese e grading a cura di Nicolò Caneparo
Montaggio a cura di Nicole Preacco
Una produzione a cura di Nickland Media

#minimarciagranparadiso #MiniMGP #Cogne

videos.title: MARCIAGRANPARADISO 2025 - Antoine Cyr with his fellows from Nordiq Canada Cross-Country Ski Team
videos.description:
Antoine Cyr, after competing in the Cross-Country Skiing World Cup stage in Cogne alongside his teammates Katherine Stewart-Jones, Olivia Bouffard Nesbitt, Rémi Drolet, Olivier Léveillé, and Pierre Grall-Johnson, will once again take center stage on the trails of the Aosta Valley, participating in the ultimate long-distance race, the MarciaGranParadiso.

The entire Nordiq Canada Ski Team stayed in Cogne for a week of training and, with great pleasure and pride, decided to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the MarciaGranParadiso alongside all the other participants.  

This legendary long-distance cross-country ski race first began writing its history back in 1975.

-----

BACKGROUND MUSIC:
Boogie Breakin' by MooveKa from Artlist.io
Creator Pro License Number: Wu7mK6 
License owner: Nicolò Caneparo

videos.title: MARCIAGRANPARADISO 2018 - The Film - Skating
videos.description:
Bonaldi and Brocard dominate the opening skating of the 36th Marciagranparadiso

159 racers at the start coming from 10 different nations.

Many racers not only from Europe but also a representer from Australia: Marciagranparadiso 2018 makes a great opening with a 25 kilometres skating which seems it has been very international.
Under the point of view of weather condition the day was absolutely perfect, ideal to open the three-day event dedicated to cross-country skiing in Cogne. Tomorrow it’s the turn of Minimarcia, the challenge for the young skiers and on Sunday the Marciagranparadiso Classic, on a 25 and 45 kilometres courses.
On the technical side, the race took place on a good snow, quite fast. The start was on time at 10:00 am  with the sun that was shining just above the mountains lighting up the ski stadium. In a hour and ten minutes Sergio Bonaldi (C.S. Esercito) nailed the first place on the finish line, in front of Jean Luc Perron (Ski Valtournenche) by only 28” and Simone Paredi (C.S. Esercito) by 48”. Among the ladies Elisa Brocard got the win: the olympic skier from Gressan (C.S. Esercito) has completed the race, faced as a training for the Olympics, in a hour, 17’ and 49”, in front of Chiara Caminada (Ski Nordico Torino) 8’:35”, and Lea Cavagnet (S.C. Gran Paradiso A.S.D.) 24’:16”.
«Every year Marciagranparadiso, with lots of international participants, becomes a fundamental way to promote the spot under the touristic point of view but also to present and remember all the future events which will take place here in Cogne. This year for example on March 3rd and 4th there will be OPA Cup and OPA Games» – Raffaella Carlin, director of Cogne Touristic Bureau, said.

Interviews, Camera & Editing: Nicolò Caneparo
A production by: Nickland Media

#marciagranparadiso #mgp2018 #nicklandmedia

videos.title: Nordic Ski Marcia Gran Paradiso 2 Feb 2020
25Km Skating Marciagranparadiso Euroloppet 2020
videos.description:
Wladimir Cuaz and Chiara Caminada have won the 37th Marciagranparadiso Skating

An amazing setting and a postcard day in Cogne, where this morning the 37th edition of the MarciaGranParadiso, part of Euroloppet circuit, has started. Clear sky, perfect track and warm temperature welcomed the participants of the 25 kilometers free technique who challenged each other on the ring designed between Valnontey and Epinel, passing through the Prati di Sant’Orso. Wladimir Cuaz and Chiara Caminada are the winners.

Very high rhythm since the beginning with a group of ten competitors who made the pace. Wladmir Cuaz, a cyclist who loves tight skis, soon took the lead of operations and, after breaking away, managed the last few kilometers. The skier from Aosta Valley won the skating race with a solo action, covering the distance in 1h:21':03" and leaving the Norwegian Kristian Braathen at 4':59". Third step of the podium for the Frenchman Michel Thomas, with a gap of 5’02 ". Fourth place for Alberto Peracino (Valsavarenche; +5':43") and fifth for Denis Trento (CSE; +7':20").
 
Same script in the women's race. Chiara Caminada, who lives in Cogne but fights for the Trentino company Robinson Ski Team, immediately grabbed the first position and she never gave up. She backed to the Prati di Sant’Orso after 1h:33':26" of effort, ahead of Elena Serdtseva, delayed by 6':35", with the third step of the podium for Michela Comola from Valle d'Aosta, at 7':37" from the top. Fourth place for the Australian Hannah Price (+15':39") and fifth for the Finnish Malin Stenman (+21':14").
 
For many, however, the MarciaGranParadiso skating race was an opportunity to discover the beauty of the Cogne valley and to ski on the slopes without thinking too much about the time trial.

Camera and editing by Nicolò Caneparo
Interviews by Claudine Brunod, Nicolò Caneparo
Cover photo by Andrea Chiericato
A production by Nickland Media

#marciagranparadiso #mgp2020 #euroloppet
